explanation: This is the original true positive command and is not a bypass. It is included for reference.
The command is modified by splitting 'calc.exe' into 'calc .exe', which may still be parsed correctly by the executable in some contexts.
Backticks are used to enclose the binary name, which may be parsed correctly by the executable while altering the command structure.
The binary path is modified to use a relative path ('.\calc.exe') instead of just 'calc.exe', which may still be valid depending on the context.
A comment is appended to the command line. While this is not standard in Windows command lines, it may be tolerated in some parsing contexts.
A comment marker is added without content, which may be ignored by the parser while still triggering the rule.
An escape character is added at the end of 'calc.exe', which may be ignored by the parser but still execute the intended binary.
